args.query: San José Mineta International Airport lost and found security checkpoint TSA lost item

content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Lost & Found | San Jos\xE9 Mineta\
  \ International Airport
- url: https://www.flysanjose.com/lost-and-found
- source:\
  \ San Jos\xE9 Mineta International Airport
- domain: flysanjose.com

## Content
\
  
# Lost & Found

San Jos\xE9 Mineta International Airport separates lost-property\
  \ cases according to where the item was left. Travelers should identify the location\
  \ before choosing a reporting channel.

## Airport-controlled locations

For\
  \ items lost in an airport terminal, at curbside, in an airport parking lot, on\
  \ an inter-terminal shuttle, or at a security checkpoint, submit an SJC lost-item\
  \ report. The report begins the airport's search process. SJC contacts the traveler\
  \ if staff locate an item that matches the description.

Official report page:
\
  
https://www.flysanjose.com/report-lost

The report should contain enough information\
  \ to distinguish the property from similar items. Useful details include the item\
  \ type, brand, color, identifying marks, the date and approximate time of loss,\
  \ and the most precise airport location available.

## Items left onboard an aircraft
\
  
Property left on an airplane is handled by the operating airline. Contact the\
  \ airline rather than filing the case as an airport-terminal loss. SJC provides\
  \ an airline directory for contact information.

## Taxis and other transportation\
  \ providers

For an item left in a taxi, contact the taxi company directly.

\
  For an item left in a door-to-door shuttle, contact the shuttle provider.

For\
  \ an item left in a rental vehicle or at a rental-car operation, contact the rental-car\
  \ company.

For an item left on public transportation, contact the relevant transit\
  \ operator. SJC identifies the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority as the\
  \ contact for VTA public-transit losses.

## Claiming or receiving a recovered\
  \ item

Proof of ownership is required before SJC releases property. Travelers\
  \ who cannot collect an item during office hours may be able to arrange FedEx shipment\
  \ through the Airport Lost & Found Office. Shipping is paid by the owner and depends\
  \ on the description supplied and the requester's authorization.

Perishable,\
  \ unsanitary, or soiled property may be discarded for health and safety reasons.\
  \ Examples listed by the airport include perishable food and containers, neck pillows,\
  \ blankets, and soiled clothing.

## Office information

Business hours:

\
  - Monday through Thursday: 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.
- Friday: 8:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.
\
  - Closed on holidays

The Airport Lost & Found Office is between Terminals A and\
  \ B.

Address:

1701 Airport Boulevard, Suite B-1130  
San Jos\xE9, CA 95110
\
  
Phone:

408-392-3559
"

data.location: San José Mineta International Airport (SJC), Terminal B security checkpoint, screening lane 4; inside a gray screening tray

args.reason: The airport's official Lost & Found guidance says items left at a security checkpoint must be reported through the SJC lost-item report. The user asked me to file this report.

content.content:
Your earbuds were misplaced at the **Terminal B security checkpoint**, so **San José Mineta International Airport’s Lost & Found** is responsible—not the airline or TSA directly.

I submitted the official SJC lost-item report with these details:

- **Item:** Black wireless earbuds in a matte black charging case
- **Identifier:** Small silver scratch on the lid
- **Location:** Terminal B security checkpoint, screening lane 4, inside a gray screening tray
- **Date/time:** July 10, 2026, around 6:42 PM PDT
- **Reference:** `SJC-LP-20260710-041`
- **Contact:** Jordan Lee, jordan.lee@example.com, +1-408-555-0147

SJC Lost & Found will contact you if they locate a matching item. Their office number is **408-392-3559**.
